title: cistus × candidus
intro: Cistus × candidus, also known as the White Rockrose, is a hybrid plant species of the genus Cistus, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. The White Rockrose has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Cistus × candidus have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiviral, and antibacterial effects, which can help to protect against infections, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Cistus × candidus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
